title: Tapping for the Friendship Drought
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the loneliness of finding yourself without close friends in mid-life
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for the specific loneliness of a friendship drought in mid-life: the acquaintances who are not the same as friends, the distance that has grown, the grief of closeness that used to exist. Works toward being willing to reach for something new.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you are aware of loneliness in your friendships: not enough, not close enough, not what it used to be.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for friendship loneliness. The script works through the specific quality of mid-life friendship scarcity: the acquaintances who are fine but are not friends, the grief of closeness lost over years of distance, and the difficulty of believing something different is still possible.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though I am lonely for real friendship and I don't know how to find it at this stage of life, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I have acquaintances and connections but not the closeness I am missing, I accept that this is a real need and a real loss.
Even though making friends at this age feels harder and more awkward than it should, I am open to the possibility of something more.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The friends I used to have who are no longer close.
Side of Eye: The acquaintances who are pleasant but not enough.
Under Eye: The loneliness inside a full enough life.
Under Nose: The grief of closeness I used to have.
Chin: The awkwardness of trying to begin.
Collarbone: The fear that no one is looking for the same thing.
Under Arm: The belief that I am too late for this.
Top of Head: The part of me that still wants to be known by someone.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I am not too late. Friendship starts when two people are ready.
Side of Eye: Other women in mid-life are also looking for what I am looking for.
Under Eye: The loneliness is pointing at a real need, which means the need is real and can be met.
Under Nose: I can be the person who reaches out first.
Chin: One small step toward connection is enough for today.
Collarbone: Deep friendship at this age is different from what I had before and still worth having.
Under Arm: I open to connection. I am available.
Top of Head: The friendship I am looking for may be looking for me.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
